manual_summary: 1. **Access the ACK Cluster:** - Use `kubectl` to authenticate and connect to your Alibaba Cloud ACK Kubernetes cluster. 2. **List All PodSecurityPolicies:** - Run: `kubectl get psp` to see all PSPs configured in the cluster. 3. **Inspect Each PSP for allowedCapabilities:** - For each PSP, run: `kubectl get psp <psp-name> -o yaml` - Examine the `allowedCapabilities` field in the output. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Ensure that `allowedCapabilities` is either not present or is set to an empty array (`[]`) in all PSPs. - If any PSP has `allowedCapabilities` set to anything other than an empty array, the cluster is non-compliant. 5. **Document Findings:** - Record which PSPs were inspected and their `allowedCapabilities` settings for audit evidence.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
description: There	are	a	variety	of	CNI	plugins	available	for	Kubernetes.	If	the	CNI	in	use	does	not support	Network	Policies	it	may	not	be	possible	to	effectively	restrict	traffic	in	the	cluster.
rationale: Kubernetes	network	policies	are	enforced	by	the	CNI	plugin	in	use.	As	such	it	is	important to	ensure	that	the	CNI	plugin	supports	both	Ingress	and	Egress	network	policies. Impact: None
audit: Review	the	documentation	of	CNI	plugin	in	use	by	the	cluster,	and	confirm	that	it	supports Ingress	and	Egress	network	policies.
remediation: If	the	CNI	plugin	in	use	does	not	support	network	policies,	consideration	should	be	given	to making	use	of	a	different	plugin,	or	finding	an	alternate	mechanism	for	restricting	traffic	in the	Kubernetes	cluster. Default	Value: This	will	depend	on	the	CNI	plugin	in	use. CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Version	7 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 233	|	P a g e

CIS	Controls: Version	6 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Version	7 9	Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services Limitation	and	Control	of	Network	Ports,	Protocols,	and	Services 233	|	P a g e
justification: While some aspects, such as identifying the CNI plugin in use, can be partially automated via Kubernetes API queries or Alibaba Cloud ACK APIs, the core requirement of this control is to confirm whether the CNI plugin supports both ingress and egress network policies. This information is not exposed via Alibaba Cloud APIs or standard Kubernetes API responses; it requires reviewing the plugin's official documentation or release notes. Therefore, a fully automated check is not feasible for this control.
manual_summary: 1. **Identify the CNI Plugin in Use:** - Access the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console for the target Kubernetes cluster. - Navigate to the cluster details and review the networking section to determine the CNI plugin deployed (e.g., Terway, Flannel, Calico). - Alternatively, use `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` and look for pods related to known CNI plugins (e.g., calico-node, flannel, terway-eni). 2. **Review Official Documentation:** - Locate the official documentation or GitHub repository for the identified CNI plugin. - Specifically search for sections on "Network Policy" support, ensuring both ingress and egress policies are mentioned as supported features. 3. **Verify Compliance:** - Confirm that the CNI plugin explicitly states support for both ingress and egress network policies. - Document the source (URL, documentation version) where this support is confirmed. 4. **Record Findings:** - Note the CNI plugin name, version, and documentation reference in your compliance records. - If the plugin does not support both policy types, flag as non-compliant and recommend remediation.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure that all Namespaces have Network Policies defined
description: Kubernetes	supports	mounting	secrets	as	data	volumes	or	as	environment	variables. Minimize	the	use	of	environment	variable	secrets.
rationale: It	is	reasonably	common	for	application	code	to	log	out	its	environment	(particularly	in	the event	of	an	error).	This	will	include	any	secret	values	passed	in	as	environment	variables, so	secrets	can	easily	be	exposed	to	any	user	or	entity	who	has	access	to	the	logs. Impact: Application	code	which	expects	to	read	secrets	in	the	form	of	environment	variables	would need	modification
audit: Run	the	following	command	to	find	references	to	objects	which	use	environment	variables defined	from	secrets. kubectl get all -o jsonpath='{range .items[?(@..secretKeyRef)]} {.kind} {.metadata.name} {"\n"}{end}' -A
remediation: If	possible,	rewrite	application	code	to	read	secrets	from	mounted	secret	files,	rather	than from	environment	variables. Default	Value: By	default,	secrets	are	not	defined References: 236	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/#using-secrets Additional	Information: Mounting	secrets	as	volumes	has	the	additional	benefit	that	secret	values	can	be	updated without	restarting	the	pod CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 237	|	P a g e
impact: Application	code	which	expects	to	read	secrets	in	the	form	of	environment	variables	would need	modification
references: 236	|	P a g e 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/#using-secrets Additional	Information: Mounting	secrets	as	volumes	has	the	additional	benefit	that	secret	values	can	be	updated without	restarting	the	pod CIS	Controls: Version	7 14.4	Encrypt	All	Sensitive	Information	in	Transit Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	in	transit. 14.8	Encrypt	Sensitive	Information	at	Rest Encrypt	all	sensitive	information	at	rest	using	a	tool	that	requires	a	secondary authentication	mechanism	not	integrated	into	the	operating	system,	in	order	to	access	the information. 237	|	P a g e
justification: The audit requires verifying that all Kubernetes namespaces have network policies defined. This information is only accessible via the Kubernetes API (using kubectl or similar tools) and is not exposed through Alibaba Cloud APIs, Aliyun CLI, or Alibaba Cloud Config. Alibaba Cloud does not provide a native API or managed service to enumerate or validate Kubernetes network policies at the namespace level across ACK clusters. Therefore, this check cannot be fully automated using Alibaba Cloud’s native tools.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the Kubernetes Cluster:** - Use `kubectl` with appropriate credentials (kubeconfig) to connect to your ACK cluster. 2. **List Network Policies Across All Namespaces:** - Run: `kubectl get networkpolicies --all-namespaces` - This command lists all defined network policies in every namespace. 3. **Identify Namespaces Without Network Policies:** - Run: `kubectl get namespaces` to list all namespaces. - For each namespace, check if it appears in the output of the previous command. - Any namespace not listed does not have a network policy defined. 4. **Verify Compliance:** - Ensure every namespace has at least one network policy. - Document any namespace lacking a network policy as non-compliant. 5. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - All namespaces should have at least one network policy that restricts traffic according to the principle of least privilege.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider external secret storage
description: Configure	Image	Provenance	for	your	deployment.
rationale: Kubernetes	supports	plugging	in	provenance	rules	to	accept	or	reject	the	images	in	your deployments.	You	could	configure	such	rules	to	ensure	that	only	approved	images	are deployed	in	the	cluster. Impact: You	need	to	regularly	maintain	your	provenance	configuration	based	on	container	image updates.
audit: Review	the	pod	definitions	in	your	cluster	and	verify	that	image	provenance	is	configured as	appropriate.
remediation: Follow	the	Kubernetes	documentation	and	setup	image	provenance. Default	Value: By	default,	image	provenance	is	not	set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/image-provenance.md 3. https://hub.docker.com/r/dnurmi/anchore-toolbox/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22888 239	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security Version	7 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security 240	|	P a g e 5.6 General Policies These	policies	relate	to	general	cluster	management	topics,	like	namespace	best	practices and	policies	applied	to	pod	objects	in	the	cluster.
impact: You	need	to	regularly	maintain	your	provenance	configuration	based	on	container	image updates.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/admission-controllers/#imagepolicywebhook 2. https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/design- proposals/image-provenance.md 3. https://hub.docker.com/r/dnurmi/anchore-toolbox/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/22888 239	|	P a g e Additional	Information: This	control	cannot	be	audited	and	modified	in	ACK	managed	clusters. CIS	Controls: Version	6 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security Version	7 18	Application	Software	Security Application	Software	Security 240	|	P a g e 5.6 General Policies These	policies	relate	to	general	cluster	management	topics,	like	namespace	best	practices and	policies	applied	to	pod	objects	in	the	cluster.
justification: While some aspects of Kubernetes configuration can be queried via the Kubernetes API or tools like `kubectl`, Alibaba Cloud APIs (Aliyun CLI, Alibaba Cloud CLI, Alibaba Cloud Config) do not natively expose or verify image provenance configurations such as ImagePolicyWebhook or custom admission controllers. These provenance settings are cluster-level Kubernetes configurations, not Alibaba Cloud-managed resources, and require direct inspection of cluster YAMLs or admission controller setups. Therefore, a manual review is necessary to confirm compliance with this control.
manual_summary: 1. **Collect Pod Definitions:** - Access your Alibaba Cloud ACK (Kubernetes) cluster via `kubectl` or the Alibaba Cloud console. - Export all current pod definitions and relevant admission controller configurations (e.g., `kubectl get pods --all-namespaces -o yaml` and `kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations,mutatingwebhookconfigurations -o yaml`). 2. **Review Image Provenance Configuration:** - Examine the exported YAML files for evidence of image provenance enforcement, such as the use of `ImagePolicyWebhook` or other admission controllers that enforce image signature or provenance checks. - Confirm that these policies reference approved image registries, signatures, or CI/CD pipelines as required by your organization. 3. **Verify Consistency and Coverage:** - Ensure that image provenance policies are consistently applied across all namespaces and workloads. - Check that there are no exceptions or bypasses that would allow unapproved images. 4. **Expected Secure Configuration:** - Only images from trusted sources (e.g., signed images, approved registries) are permitted. - ImagePolicyWebhook or equivalent is enabled and configured according to organizational policy. 5. **Confirm Compliance:** - Document findings and retain evidence (YAML snippets, screenshots) showing that image provenance controls are in place and enforced.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Create administrative boundaries between resources using namespaces
description: Use	namespaces	to	isolate	your	Kubernetes	objects.
rationale: Limiting	the	scope	of	user	permissions	can	reduce	the	impact	of	mistakes	or	malicious activities.	A	Kubernetes	namespace	allows	you	to	partition	created	resources	into	logically named	groups.	Resources	created	in	one	namespace	can	be	hidden	from	other	namespaces. By	default,	each	resource	created	by	a	user	in	Kubernetes	cluster	runs	in	a	default namespace,	called	default.	You	can	create	additional	namespaces	and	attach	resources	and users	to	them.	You	can	use	Kubernetes	Authorization	plugins	to	create	policies	that segregate	access	to	namespace	resources	between	different	users. Impact: You	need	to	switch	between	namespaces	for	administration.
audit: Run	the	below	command	and	review	the	namespaces	created	in	the	cluster. kubectl get namespaces Ensure	that	these	namespaces	are	the	ones	you	need	and	are	adequately	administered	as per	your	requirements.
remediation: Follow	the	documentation	and	create	namespaces	for	objects	in	your	deployment	as	you need	them. 241	|	P a g e Default	Value: By	default,	Kubernetes	starts	with	two	initial	namespaces: 1. default	-	The	default	namespace	for	objects	with	no	other	namespace 2. kube-system	-	The	namespace	for	objects	created	by	the	Kubernetes	system References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with- objects/namespaces/ 2. http://blog.kubernetes.io/2016/08/security-best-practices-kubernetes- deployment.html CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Version	7 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 242	|	P a g e
impact: You	need	to	switch	between	namespaces	for	administration.
references: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with- objects/namespaces/ 2. http://blog.kubernetes.io/2016/08/security-best-practices-kubernetes- deployment.html CIS	Controls: Version	6 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Version	7 14	Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know Controlled	Access	Based	on	the	Need	to	Know 242	|	P a g e
justification: While the retrieval of Kubernetes namespaces (e.g., via `kubectl get namespaces` or Alibaba Cloud ACK APIs) can be automated, the core of this control is to ensure that namespaces are created and administered according to organizational policy and security requirements. This assessment requires human judgment to determine if the namespace structure aligns with business needs and security boundaries, which cannot be fully automated by any Alibaba Cloud API or CLI. Automation can only provide the raw data, not the compliance decision.
manual_summary: 1. Use the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console or connect to your cluster using `kubectl`: - In terminal: Run `kubectl get namespaces` to list all namespaces in the cluster. - Or, navigate to the Alibaba Cloud ACK Console > Clusters > [Your Cluster] > Namespaces. 2. Review the list of namespaces and compare them against your organization's documented namespace policy and security requirements. 3. Verify that each namespace serves a clear, documented purpose and that resource isolation is enforced as intended (e.g., no unnecessary or unused namespaces, no policy violations). 4. Confirm that namespace usage aligns with security best practices for access separation and resource isolation. 5. Document your findings and confirm compliance or note any deviations for remediation.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: The default namespace should not be used
description: Kubernetes	provides	a	default	namespace,	where	objects	are	placed	if	no	namespace	is specified	for	them.	Placing	objects	in	this	namespace	makes	application	of	RBAC	and	other controls	more	difficult.
rationale: Resources	in	a	Kubernetes	cluster	should	be	segregated	by	namespace,	to	allow	for	security controls	to	be	applied	at	that	level	and	to	make	it	easier	to	manage	resources. Impact: None
audit: Run	this	command	to	list	objects	in	default	namespace kubectl get all The	only	entries	there	should	be	system	managed	resources	such	as	the	kubernetes	service
remediation: Ensure	that	namespaces	are	created	to	allow	for	appropriate	segregation	of	Kubernetes resources	and	that	all	new	resources	are	created	in	a	specific	namespace. Default	Value: Unless	a	namespace	is	specific	on	object	creation,	the	default	namespace	will	be	used CIS	Controls: Version	6 5.1	Minimize	And	Sparingly	Use	Administrative	Privileges Minimize	administrative	privileges	and	only	use	administrative	accounts	when	they	are 247	|	P a g e required.	Implement	focused	auditing	on	the	use	of	administrative	privileged	functions	and monitor	for	anomalous	behavior. Version	7 5.1	Establish	Secure	Configurations Maintain	documented,	standard	security	configuration	standards	for	all	authorized operating	systems	and	software. 6 Managed services This	section	consists	of	security	recommendations	for	the	direct	configuration	of Kubernetes	managed	service	components,	namely,	Alibaba	Cloud	Container	Service	for Kubernetes	(ACK).	These	recommendations	are	directly	applicable	for	features	which	exist only	as	part	of	a	managed	service. 248	|	P a g e 6.1 Image Registry and Image Scanning This	section	contains	recommendations	relating	to	container	image	registries	and	securing images	in	those	registries,	such	as	Alibaba	Cloud	Container	Registry	(ACR).
impact: None
justification: While Alibaba Cloud APIs and CLI tools (such as Aliyun CLI and `kubectl`) can automate the retrieval of all resources in the default namespace, the critical compliance determination—distinguishing between system-managed and user-created resources—requires human judgment and contextual understanding. There is no definitive API property or automated logic that can reliably classify every resource as "system" or "non-system" without manual review, especially as system resources may vary by cluster version and configuration.
manual_summary: 1. Ensure you have access to the target Alibaba Cloud ACK cluster and that `kubectl` is configured with appropriate credentials. 2. Run the following command to list all resources in the default namespace: ``` kubectl get all --namespace=default ``` 3. Review the output and identify all resources present. 4. Compare the listed resources against the expected system-managed resources (e.g., the default `kubernetes` service). 5. Confirm that no user-created workloads (such as Deployments, Pods, Services, etc.) are present in the default namespace. 6. Document any non-system resources found and escalate for remediation. 7. Mark the control as compliant only if the default namespace contains exclusively system-managed resources.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider ACK Sandboxed-Container for running untrusted workloads
description: Use	ACK	Sandboxed-Container	to	restrict	untrusted	workloads	as	an	additional	layer	of protection	when	running	in	a	multi-tenant	environment.
rationale: Sandboxed-Container	provides	an	alternative	to	the	Docker	runtime.	It	allows	you	to	run applications	in	a	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machine	that	has	a	dedicated	kernel. This	enhances	resource	isolation	and	improves	security.	Compared	with	Docker, SandboxedContainer	has	the	following	benefits: • Strong	isolation	based	on	sandboxed	and	lightweight	virtual	machines. • Network	Attached	Storage	(NAS)	file	systems	and	Alibaba	Cloud	disks	can	be mounted	to	Sandboxed-Container.	This	provides	the	same	storage	performance	as storage	volumes	that	are	mounted	to	the	host. • Compatibility	with	runC	in	terms	of	application	management. • High	performance	that	corresponds	to	90%	performance	of	applications	based	on runC. • The	same	user	experience	as	runC	in	terms	of	logging,	monitoring,	and	storage. • Support	for	RuntimeClass. • Easy	to	use	with	limited	expertise	that	is	required	to	use	virtual	machines. • Higher	stability	than	that	provided	by	Kata	Containers.
audit: Remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the	page. 4. On	the	Select	Cluster	Template	page,	find	Standard	Managed	Cluster	and	click	Create in	the	Standard	Managed	Cluster	section. 275	|	P a g e 5. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	configure	the	cluster	and	set	the	parameters	as required	in	the	following	list: • Zone:	Select	a	zone	where	Elastics	Compute	Service	(ECS)	Bare	Metal	instances	are deployed.	Sandboxed	containers	support	only	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Kubernetes	Version:	Select	1.18.8-aliyun.1	or	later. • Container	Runtime:	Select	Sandboxed-Container. • Worker	Instance:	Add	worker	nodes	by	creating	new	ECS	instances. • Billing	Method:	Select	the	subscription	billing	method. • Instance	Type:	Select	ECS	Bare	Metal	instances. • Mount	Data	Disk:	Mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	200	GiB.	We	recommend	that	you mount	a	data	disk	of	at	least	1	TB. • Operating	system:	By	default,	the	AliyunLinux	operating	system	is	used.	You	cannot change	the	operating	system. Default	Value: By	default,	Sandboxed	container	is	disabled References: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
references: 1. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/142151.htm 2. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/140541.htm 3. https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/160288.htm CIS	Controls: Version	7 18.9	Separate	Production	and	Non-Production	Systems Maintain	separate	environments	for	production	and	nonproduction	systems.	Developers should	not	have	unmonitored	access	to	production	environments. 276	|	P a g e
justification: While certain aspects of this control—such as verifying the container runtime for a cluster—can be automated using Alibaba Cloud CLI or API, the core requirement is to "consider" and apply ACK Sandboxed-Container specifically for "untrusted workloads." This involves subjective judgment about workload trust levels, business context, and security policy, which cannot be determined programmatically. There is no API or CLI method to automatically identify which workloads are "untrusted" or to confirm that Sandboxed-Container is being used for them according to organizational policy.
manual_summary: 1. **Access the ACK Console:** - Log in to the Alibaba Cloud ACK console at https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. - In the left navigation pane, click "Clusters." 2. **Review Cluster Configuration:** - Select the relevant Kubernetes cluster. - Check the "Container Runtime" setting to verify if "Sandboxed-Container" is enabled. - Confirm the cluster is running on ECS Bare Metal instances and is using Kubernetes version 1.18.8-aliyun.1 or later. 3. **Assess Workload Assignment:** - Review organizational security policies and documentation to determine which workloads are classified as "untrusted." - Cross-reference these workloads with the cluster(s) or node pools configured to use Sandboxed-Container. 4. **Confirm Compliance:** - Ensure that all untrusted workloads are deployed exclusively to clusters or node pools with Sandboxed-Container enabled. - Document evidence (screenshots, policy references, cluster settings) for audit purposes.

source: CIS ALIBABA CLOUD CONTAINER SERVICE FOR KUBERNETES (ACK) BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Consider use service account token volume projection
description: Use	service	account	token	volume	projection	to	enhance	security	when	you	use	service accounts.
rationale: The	feature	of	service	account	token	volume	projection	enables	the	kubelet	to	request	and store	the	token	on	behalf	of	the	pod,	and	allows	you	to	configure	token	properties	such	as the	audience	and	validity	duration.	As	the	token	approaches	expiration,	the	kubelet proactively	rotates	the	token	if	it	is	older	than	80	percent	of	its	time	to	live	(TTL)	or	if	it	is older	than	24	hours.
audit: Using	Command	Line: 1. Log	in	to	the	master	node	of	an	existing	dedicated	cluser	which	has	enabled	service account	token	volume	projection 2. Use	the	following	command	to	create	a	serviceaccount: kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: ServiceAccount metadata: name: xxxxx EOF  3. Use	the	following	command	to	create	a	pod	which	using	the	projected serviceaccount token: kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: nginx spec: containers: 279	|	P a g e - image: nginx name: nginx volumeMounts: - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/tokens #customized volumemounts path for the token file name: vault-token serviceAccountName: xxxxx volumes: - name: vault-token projected: sources: - serviceAccountToken: path: vault-token  #customized audie expirationSeconds: 7200  #customized token expiration seconds audience: xxxxx    #customized audience which used to validate the tokens at the API server side  4. When	pod	running,	log	into	the	pod	and	make	sure	there	is	a	projected	token	file generated under	the	specific	path	defined	the	pod	spec	(e.g.	/var/run/secrets/tokens/vault- token	in the	sample	before).
remediation: For	create	cluster: 1. Log	on	to	the	ACK	console	https://cs.console.aliyun.com/. 2. In	the	left-side	navigation	pane,	click	Clusters. 3. On	the	Clusters	page,	click	Create	Kubernetes	Cluster	in	the	upper-right	corner	of the page. 4. On	the	ACK	managed	edition	tab,	click	the	Show	Advanced	Options,	and	select	the Enable check	box	to	enable	service	account	token	volume	projection. 5. Configure	the	the	parameters	of	service_account_issuer	and	api_audiences	upon	to the needed. justification: While some aspects of Kubernetes cluster configuration can be queried via APIs or scripted using kubectl, the specific verification of service account token volume projection requires direct inspection of pod and service account manifests and their mounted volumes. Alibaba Cloud's APIs and CLI do not natively expose or validate the presence and configuration of projected service account tokens at the required granularity. The audit steps necessitate manual creation and inspection of resources within the cluster, which cannot be fully automated through Alibaba Cloud's native tools.
manual_summary: 1. Log in to the master node of the ACK cluster where service account token volume projection is expected to be enabled. 2. Create a ServiceAccount using the following command: ```sh kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: ServiceAccount metadata: name: test-sa EOF ``` 3. Create a Pod that uses the ServiceAccount and projects the token: ```sh kubectl apply -f - <<EOF apiVersion: v1 kind: Pod metadata: name: test-nginx spec: containers: - image: nginx name: nginx volumeMounts: - mountPath: /var/run/secrets/tokens name: vault-token serviceAccountName: test-sa volumes: - name: vault-token projected: sources: - serviceAccountToken: path: vault-token expirationSeconds: 3600 audience: api EOF ``` 4. After the pod is running, exec into the pod and verify the presence and properties of the projected token at `/var/run/secrets/tokens/vault-token`. 5. Confirm that the token is valid, has the correct audience, and is rotated as expected. 6. Document the findings for compliance evidence.
